verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted CAP-family protein with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or lipidation. It is produced by epididymal epithelium, secreted into the epididymal lumen, and adsorbs onto maturing spermatozoa. A tightly bound fraction remains on the sperm surface even after high-ionic-strength washing and persists through capacitation, qualifying as wash-resistant surface association. However, this surface display is entirely restricted to sperm cells in the male germline/reproductive tract — there is no evidence of surface expression on somatic cells. This maps to contextual/tissue_restricted_surface: the protein reaches the outer leaflet of sperm plasma membrane in a narrow germline/gamete lineage via stable post-translational adsorption in a specialized secretory compartment.
